props.heading: Match Desk: Connect those who need help with donors and volunteers who can help
props.description: Use one simple request form. We triage every submission into the right lane—Need Help, Offer Help, or Partner Request—and connect people quickly with accountable follow-through.

highlights.text: Two-sided intake: request support or offer support
24-hour first response target for all valid submissions
Priority triage for urgent needs and high-capacity offers

props.heading: How the Match Desk works (simple and practical)
props.body:
Step 1: Submit one form and clearly choose your intent: Need Help or Offer Help.

Step 2: Our coordinators triage requests by urgency, location, and support type.

Step 3: We connect both sides with clear next actions, timelines, and a single accountability owner.

props.heading: Two-sided matching lanes
props.description: Whether you need support or want to provide it, each lane is designed for quick routing and clear expectations.

features.title: Need Help Lane
features.description: Families, individuals, or charities can request support with urgency level, location, and type of help needed.

features.title: Offer Help Lane
features.description: Donors and volunteers can offer funds, skills, or time with availability, capacity, and preferred cause area.

features.title: Coordinator Triage
features.description: Each request is reviewed for fit, urgency, and safety so matching decisions are practical and responsible.

features.title: Accountable Follow-Up
features.description: Both sides receive clear next steps, timeline updates, and one coordinator contact for continuity.

props.heading: 48-hour connection workflow
props.description: A practical process built for speed, quality, and trust.

items.title: Submit Structured Request
items.description: You submit intent, support type, location, capacity/urgency, and contact details in one form.

items.title: Coordinator Triage
items.description: Our team validates details, prioritizes urgent cases, and prepares a best-fit matching shortlist.

items.title: First Connection
items.description: We introduce qualified donors/volunteers to verified needs or route partner requests with clear expectations.

items.title: Track and Close Loop
items.description: We confirm progress, share updates, and document outcomes to keep future matching faster and smarter.

props.heading: What can be matched through this website
props.description: Choose the exact support format so we can connect both sides quickly.

features.title: Financial Support Match
features.description: Connect donors to verified needs with budget range, intended use, and reporting expectations.

features.title: Skills-Based Volunteer Match
features.description: Route professionals to charities needing expertise in teaching, design, operations, communications, or tech.

features.title: Time-Based Volunteer Match
features.description: Match volunteers by weekly availability, on-site or remote preference, and program urgency.

features.title: Urgent Need Routing
features.description: Flag emergency or high-priority requests so coordinators can escalate and respond first.

props.heading: Matching FAQ
props.description: Everything needed to connect both sides with less friction.

faqs.question: How fast do you respond to a new request?
faqs.answer: Our target is a first response within 24 hours for complete submissions. Urgent requests are triaged first.

faqs.question: Can this form be used by both people needing help and people offering help?
faqs.answer: Yes. Select your intent clearly in the form and we route to the right matching lane.

faqs.question: What details improve matching quality?
faqs.answer: Intent, location, support type, urgency or capacity, and preferred timeline are the most useful fields.

faqs.question: How are urgent requests handled?
faqs.answer: Urgent requests are flagged during triage and moved to priority routing with faster coordinator follow-up.

faqs.question: Can donors specify preferred causes or boundaries?
faqs.answer: Yes. Donors can provide cause preferences, budget range, and any constraints in their request.

faqs.question: Can volunteers serve remotely?
faqs.answer: Yes. Include remote preference and skills so we can route you to suitable opportunities.

faqs.question: How do charities or community groups request support?
faqs.answer: Choose Need Help in the form, include organization details, request type, and timeline.

props.heading: Submit your Match Desk request
props.description: This single form connects both sides: people/charities needing help and donors/volunteers offering help.

fields.name: intent
fields.label: Intent (Need Help / Offer Help / Partner Request)

fields.name: support_type
fields.label: Support Type (Funds / Skills / Time / Essentials / Other)

fields.name: capacity_or_urgency
fields.label: Capacity or Urgency (e.g., budget, hours/week, immediate need)

fields.name: message
fields.label: Details we should know to make the best match

props.successMessage: Request received. A Match Desk coordinator will review and respond with next steps within 24 hours.

props.heading: Ready to connect help with need?
props.description: If you can give, volunteer, or partner—or if you need urgent support—submit one match request and we’ll route it quickly.
